title: Same-lap leave-one-out peer pressure
summary: Compare each driver to other drivers on the same race lap using leave-one-out averages of tyre pressure, final-stop feasibility, and current PitStop context.
rationale: Same-race/same-lap peer features were frequently attempted and sometimes promising but failure-prone. A correctly implemented version may capture whether a car is unusually likely to pit relative to the field at the same moment.
implementation_hint: For Year-Race-LapNumber groups, compute row-aligned leave-one-out means and deltas for a small set of engineered pressure features, TyreLife, LapTime_Delta, Cumulative_Degradation, Position, and PitStop. Use transform-based group sizes and sums to avoid MultiIndex alignment failures.
expected_effect: Small AUC gain with medium-low confidence; useful as a distinct relative-context mechanism.
risk: Contemporaneous peer data must be considered available for batch prediction; if enforcing per-driver online causality only, restrict to prior-lap peer aggregates. Implementation risk is high unless groupby outputs are row-aligned.
disabled_reason: Disabled because an external duplicate-detection mechanism marked this hypothesis as duplicate of an existing hypothesis.
